notes: NetApp's proprietary NAS OS; renamed ONTAP; still core to NetApp products in 2024
Proprietary file system enabling copy-on-write snapshots, NVRAM, RAID 4 integration
Unix-native file sharing; NetApp's original protocol; still widely used
Microsoft's enhanced SMB; superseded by SMB2/SMB3; NetApp's CIFS support was key differentiator in 1996
NetApp used RAID 4 with dedicated parity disk; unusual choice enabling WAFL's write optimization
NetApp led standards effort; became industry standard for NAS backup management
Three-tier product line for workgroup to production; succeeded by FAS series
